codeReview.summary: The commit successfully bootstraps the required Flutter, Rust, and macOS shell skeletons at the mission-defined paths, but it leaves one shared service command inconsistent with the scaffolded workspace.

issues.description: The shared build command still runs `flutter build macos --project-dir "$ROOT/apps/mobile"`, but this feature scaffolded `apps/mobile` with only iOS and Android targets. Because `apps/mobile` has no `macos/` runner, the shared build command cannot succeed against this workspace, so later workers cannot fully rely on the shared service commands promised by the feature.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `foundation-worker` skill example still documents an invalid Flutter test invocation from the repo root, even though mission guidance requires running Flutter tests from `apps/mobile` (or via the shared service command). This can mislead later workers into a known failure mode.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: SKILL.md:40 shows `flutter test --concurrency=5 /repo/apps/mobile/test`; mission AGENTS.md:51 says to run `cd apps/mobile && flutter test ...` or use `.factory/services.yaml`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared build command remains out of sync with the mission architecture: it assumes a Flutter macOS target under `apps/mobile`, while the mission explicitly separates the SwiftUI macOS shell into `apps/mac-shell` and treats the Flutter app as the iOS/Android client.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/services.yaml:30 runs `flutter build macos --project-dir "$ROOT/apps/mobile"`; mission AGENTS.md requires `apps/mobile` for Flutter and `apps/mac-shell/CodexMobileCompanion.xcodeproj` for the macOS shell.

summary: Reviewed commit 4ee87dc and the worker transcript/handoff for `foundation-bootstrap-workspace`. The scaffold paths, names, and minimal stubs are in place, but the feature should be treated as failing review because the shared build service still targets a nonexistent Flutter macOS runner under `apps/mobile`, leaving the shared command set inconsistent with the scaffold it introduced.
